note: Mathlib/Order/Extension/Linear.lean:24 — 'Szpilrajn extension theorem: any partial order can be extended to a linear order', ∃ s, IsLinearOrder α s ∧ r ≤ s.
verify_note: Confirmed at Mathlib/Order/Extension/Linear.lean:25 (off-by-one from proposal, decl is real); Szpilrajn extension theorem is the canonical named result behind Wikidata's 'Linear extension' article (order extension principle), so exact is honest even though mathlib also has a type LinearExtension α in the same file.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Diam.lean:40-41 — 'The eccentricity of a vertex is the greatest distance between it and any other vertex', def eccent (G) (u) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ v, G.edist u v.
verify_note: Confirmed verbatim at Diam.lean:40-41; matches Wikidata Q65556262 description word-for-word ('the greatest distance between a specific vertex and any other vertex').

note: Mathlib/FieldTheory/AbelRuffini.lean:330-333 — docstring literally '**Abel-Ruffini Theorem** (one direction): An irreducible polynomial with a solvableByRad root has a solvable Galois group.'
verify_note: Overclaimed: the decl's own file header says 'This file proves ONE DIRECTION of the Abel-Ruffini theorem' — it's the general lemma (solvable-by-radicals root ⟹ solvable Galois group), not the degree-5 unsolvability claim in Wikidata's description ('a generic equation of degree 5 or higher cannot be solved by radicals'). The actual full statement is proved as `AbelRuffini.exists_not_solvable_by_rad` in Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/AbelRuffini.lean, docstring-labeled unqualified '**Abel-Ruffini Theorem**' — that decl, not this supporting lemma, is the exact match.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/Polynomial/MahlerMeasure.lean:81 — noncomputable def mahlerMeasure : ℝ := if p ≠ 0 then exp (p.logMahlerMeasure) else 0; file header 'Mahler measure of complex polynomials'.
verify_note: Confirmed at MahlerMeasure.lean:81; this is precisely the standard Mahler measure definition, matching Wikidata's 'measure of polynomial height'.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Enumerative/Pentagonal.lean:35 — def pentagonal (k : ℤ) : ℕ := (k * (3 * k - 1) / 2).toNat; docstring cites en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pentagonal_number directly.
verify_note: Confirmed at Pentagonal.lean:35; formula k(3k-1)/2 matches the standard/generalized pentagonal number formula from the same Wikipedia article the docstring cites as its reference.
